introduction.tts_missionControlVoice: Welcome back, operative. Before we add new power, we need to check your core system. Six sounds. No guessing. Clean signals only. Let's run a SATPIN diagnostic.
introduction.tts_missionControlVoice_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Welcome back, operative. Before we add new power, we need to check your core system. Six sounds. No guessing. Clean signals only. Let's run a SATPIN diagnostic.</voice></speak>

visual.scene: Command center reactivates. The SATPIN grid appears on screen and each letter pulses softly.
visual.sfx: low system hum with soft pulse tones across S-A-T-P-I-N

alphabet_practice.tts_ss1: First up: S. Listen: /sss/. Say it: /sss/. Keep it air only.
alphabet_practice.tts_ss1_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">First up: S. Listen: /sss/. Say it: /sss/. Keep it air only.</voice></speak>

visual.mouthCue: Teeth almost together, tongue behind teeth, air flowing out (air only).

feedback.tts_exactMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Perfect. That's exactly /sss/. Locked in.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_closeMatch: Almost—you're adding voice. It's just air: /sss/, not /zzz/. Watch the animation again.
feedback.tts_closeMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Almost—you're adding voice. It's just air: /sss/, not /zzz/. Watch the animation again.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_wrongSound: That's not /sss/. Check the screen—teeth together, air only. Try again.
feedback.tts_wrongSound_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">That's not /sss/. Check the screen—teeth together, air only. Try again.</voice></speak>

alphabet_practice.tts_ss1: Next: A. Short and sharp. /a/. Say it: /a/. Keep it quick.
alphabet_practice.tts_ss1_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Next: A. Short and sharp. /a/. Say it: /a/. Keep it quick.</voice></speak>

visual.mouthCue: Medium-open mouth, quick jaw drop, tongue stays low and flat.

feedback.tts_exactMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">That's it. Perfect /a/. Locked in.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_closeMatch: You're holding it too long. Make it quick: /a/.
feedback.tts_closeMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">You're holding it too long. Make it quick: /a/.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_wrongSound: That's 'ay' like the letter name. We need the short sound: /a/ like 'ant'.
feedback.tts_wrongSound_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">That's 'ay' like the letter name. We need the short sound: /a/ like 'ant'.</voice></speak>

alphabet_practice.tts_ss1: Next: T. Clean tap. /t/. Say it: /t/. Stop at the tap.
alphabet_practice.tts_ss1_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Next: T. Clean tap. /t/. Say it: /t/. Stop at the tap.</voice></speak>

visual.mouthCue: Tongue tip taps roof of mouth just behind teeth, then releases with a burst of air.

feedback.tts_closeMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Stop before the vowel. Just /t/.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_wrongSound: You're saying 'tuh'. Stop before the vowel—just /t/.
feedback.tts_wrongSound_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">You're saying 'tuh'. Stop before the vowel—just /t/.</voice></speak>

alphabet_practice.tts_ss1: Next: P. Air pop. /p/. Say it: /p/. No voice.
alphabet_practice.tts_ss1_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Next: P. Air pop. /p/. Say it: /p/. No voice.</voice></speak>

visual.mouthCue: Lips together, then a quick air burst. Stop immediately.

feedback.tts_closeMatch: Just /p/—don't add 'puh'. Lips pop and stop.
feedback.tts_closeMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Just /p/—don't add 'puh'. Lips pop and stop.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_wrongSound: That's /b/. P has no voice—just air. Try again.
feedback.tts_wrongSound_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">That's /b/. P has no voice—just air. Try again.</voice></speak>

alphabet_practice.tts_ss1: Next: I. Tight short sound. /i/. Say it: /i/. Quick release.
alphabet_practice.tts_ss1_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Next: I. Tight short sound. /i/. Say it: /i/. Quick release.</voice></speak>

visual.mouthCue: Slightly open mouth, tongue high in the middle, short duration.

feedback.tts_closeMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Too long. Make it quick: /i/.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_wrongSound: That's 'ee' or 'eye'. We need short /i/ like 'sit'.
feedback.tts_wrongSound_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">That's 'ee' or 'eye'. We need short /i/ like 'sit'.</voice></speak>

alphabet_practice.tts_ss1: Next: N. Nasal airflow. /nnn/. Say it: /nnn/. Use your nose.
alphabet_practice.tts_ss1_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Next: N. Nasal airflow. /nnn/. Say it: /nnn/. Use your nose.</voice></speak>

visual.mouthCue: Tongue up. Air goes through the nose (nasal buzz).

feedback.tts_closeMatch: Not enough nose sound. Tongue up, push air through your nose.
feedback.tts_closeMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Not enough nose sound. Tongue up, push air through your nose.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_wrongSound: That's /m/. N has your tongue up; M has lips closed.
feedback.tts_wrongSound_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">That's /m/. N has your tongue up; M has lips closed.</voice></speak>

feedback.tts_closeMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Say each sound. Then blend.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_wrongSound_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Say each sound. Then blend.</voice></speak>

feedback.tts_closeMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Say each sound. Then blend.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_wrongSound_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Say each sound. Then blend.</voice></speak>

introduction.tts_coach: Alright, let's get straight to it. Six sounds today. These unlock actual English words you'll see everywhere—texts, signs, homework, all of it. No fluff. Let's go.
introduction.tts_coach_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Alright, let's get straight to it. Six sounds today. These unlock actual English words you'll see everywhere—texts, signs, homework, all of it. No fluff. Let's go.</voice></speak>

alphabet_practice.tts_coach: Letter S makes /sss/. It's all air, no voice. Watch the screen—teeth almost together, tongue stays behind. Listen: /sss/. Say it: /sss/.
alphabet_practice.tts_coach_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Letter S makes /sss/. It's all air, no voice. Listen: /sss/. Say it: /sss/.</voice></speak>

visual.mouthCue: Teeth almost together, tongue stays behind; air flows out.

feedback.tts_exactMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Perfect. That's exactly /sss/. Locked in.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_closeMatch: Almost—you're adding voice. It's just air: /sss/, not /zzz/. Watch the animation again.
feedback.tts_closeMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Almost—you're adding voice. It's just air: /sss/, not /zzz/. Watch the animation again.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_wrongSound: That's not /sss/. Check the screen—teeth together, air only. Try again.
feedback.tts_wrongSound_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">That's not /sss/. Check the screen—teeth together, air only. Try again.</voice></speak>

alphabet_practice.tts_coach: Letter A makes /a/. Short and sharp. Not 'ay'—just /a/. Listen: /a/. Your turn: /a/.
alphabet_practice.tts_coach_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Letter A makes /a/. Short and sharp. Not 'ay'. Listen: /a/. Your turn: /a/.</voice></speak>

visual.mouthCue: Mouth open medium-wide, quick jaw drop, tongue stays low.

feedback.tts_closeMatch: You're holding it too long. Make it quick: /a/.
feedback.tts_closeMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">You're holding it too long. Make it quick: /a/.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_wrongSound: That's 'ay' like the letter name. We need the short sound: /a/ like 'ant'.
feedback.tts_wrongSound_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">That's 'ay' like the letter name. We need the short sound: /a/ like 'ant'.</voice></speak>

alphabet_practice.tts_coach: Letter T makes /t/. It's a quick tap—tongue up, then release. No 'tuh'. Just /t/.
alphabet_practice.tts_coach_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Letter T makes /t/. It's a quick tap. Just /t/.</voice></speak>

visual.mouthCue: Tongue tip taps roof of mouth just behind teeth, then releases with a burst of air.

feedback.tts_closeMatch: You're adding a vowel. Stop before it—just /t/.
feedback.tts_closeMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">You're adding a vowel. Stop before it—just /t/.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_wrongSound: You're saying 'tuh'. Stop before the vowel—just /t/.
feedback.tts_wrongSound_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">You're saying 'tuh'. Stop before the vowel—just /t/.</voice></speak>

alphabet_practice.tts_coach: Letter P makes /p/. Lips together, then pop. No voice, just air pressure release.
alphabet_practice.tts_coach_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Letter P makes /p/. Lips together, then pop. No voice, just air.</voice></speak>

visual.mouthCue: Lips pressed together, then pop open with air burst; no vocal vibration.

feedback.tts_closeMatch: Just /p/—don't add 'puh'. Lips pop and stop.
feedback.tts_closeMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Just /p/—don't add 'puh'. Lips pop and stop.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_wrongSound: That's /b/. P has no voice—just air. Try again.
feedback.tts_wrongSound_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">That's /b/. P has no voice—just air. Try again.</voice></speak>

alphabet_practice.tts_coach: Letter I makes /i/. Quick, tight sound. Not 'eye'—just /i/ like 'it'.
alphabet_practice.tts_coach_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Letter I makes /i/. Quick, tight sound. Not 'eye'.</voice></speak>

visual.mouthCue: Slightly open mouth, tongue high in the middle, short duration.

feedback.tts_closeMatch_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Too long. Make it quick: /i/.</voice></speak>
feedback.tts_wrongSound: That's 'ee' or 'eye'. We need short /i/ like 'sit'.
feedback.tts_wrongSound_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">That's 'ee' or 'eye'. We need short /i/ like 'sit'.</voice></speak>

alphabet_practice.tts_coach: Letter N makes /nnn/. Tongue up, sound comes through your nose. Feel the buzz in your nose. Listen: /nnn/.
alphabet_practice.tts_coach_ssml: <speak version="1.0" xml:lang="en-US" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/synthesis"><voice name="en-US-JennyNeural">Letter N makes /nnn/. Tongue up. Sound comes through your nose. Listen: /nnn/.</voice></speak>

introduction.tts_coach: Now let's check what stuck. You'll read words built from S, A, T, P, I, N. Aim for at least eight out of ten to hit mastery.

summary.tts_coach: Done. You learned six sounds: S, A, T, P, I, N. With just these six, you can read actual English words. That's not kids' stuff—that's functional literacy building. Next session, we add four more sounds, and your word count explodes. English has about 44 sounds total. You just learned 6. By the end of next lesson, you'll have 10. That's almost 25% of English phonics in two sessions. Stay sharp.
